title: racosperma coolgardiense subsp. latius
intro: Racosperma coolgardiense subsp. latius, also known as the Coolgardie pea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a shrub native to Western Australia, traditionally used by the local Aboriginal people for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as treat skin conditions and wounds. The herbal preparations of the Coolgardie pea typically involve boiling the plant in water to create a tea or infusion, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its full range of benefits and potential side effects.
